evaluation.reasoning: Candidate has 4.2 years of experience, slightly below the 5-9 year range but close enough given strong signals. Directly relevant experience: owned ranking layer for e-commerce search at Nykaa (product company), evolved from hand-tuned scoring to learning-to-rank model over 9 months. Designed relevance labeling pipeline, feature pipeline, and training/eval workflow. Improved revenue-per-search by 12% — clear production impact. Skills include Learning to Rank, BM25, Sentence Transformers, Pinecone, Weaviate, Information Retrieval, Fine-tuning LLMs, NLP — directly aligned with retrieval/ranking/search requirements. Shipped RAG-based feature and owns eval framework. Strong product-company experience (Nykaa, 50 months). Active on platform (last active April 2026), high recruiter response rate (90%), interview completion rate (93%), open to work. Notice period 30 days (acceptable). Location not specified but willing_to_relocate is false — potential concern if not in Pune/Noida/Mumbai/Hyderabad/Delhi NCR. Education: M.Sc in ML from Tier-1 institution. Missing: explicit mention of embeddings-based retrieval at scale, vector DB production ops, or A/B testing infrastructure. Experience depth is solid but not yet at senior level across all required dimensions. Behavioral signals strong: high response rate, active job seeker, product-company tenure. Not a honeypot — career history is consistent and credible.

Candidate is a full-stack Java/Node.js developer with 4.2 years at Cognizant (consulting) and Globex Inc (QA/test automation). No production experience in retrieval, ranking, search, recommendation, embeddings, vector databases, or any ML/AI systems. Skills list includes irrelevant items (Figma, Accounting, YOLO) and no evidence of retrieval/ranking work. Self-described AI experience is limited to online courses and a small RAG side project — explicitly not professional. Entire career is consulting/QA with no product-company AI/ML engineering. Redrob signals show 120-day notice period, not open to work, and low recruiter response rate (0.6). Does not meet any must-have requirements. Hard reject per non-technical career rule: no engineering ownership of ML/search/retrieval systems.

Fit score capped at 8.

Not a honeypot - career history is consistent with a junior frontend/backend engineer, no contradictions detected.
